item: Polling-storm fix: adaptive vitals cadence (2s panel-open / 6s chip-only)
summary: Field diagnostics 2026-07-01 (network preflight / debug bundle: an idle polling storm -- ~28.9k /api/scheduler/activity polls over an overnight scrape contending with the encrypted DB, F5). ROOT CAUSE: _pollVitals ran on a FIXED 2s setInterval whenever _bg (a background scrape) was true, polling BOTH /api/system/vitals AND /api/scheduler/activity every 2s for the ENTIRE multi-hour scrape -- even with the vitals panel CLOSED, when only the 'Collecting N/M' chip updates (nobody watches it sub-second). FIX (surgical, reuses the codebase's own idle-backoff idea): _vitalsCadence() returns 2000ms when the panel is OPEN (user watching live vitals) and 6000ms chip-only, and _ensureVitalsPoll() is converted from a fixed setInterval to a self-scheduling setTimeout that re-reads the cadence each tick and SNAPS back to 2s + an immediate refresh when the panel opens (toggleVitals already calls it). Cuts the closed-panel case ~3x. The airplane/network state keeps its OWN separate _adaptivePoll (unchanged), so responsiveness there is untouched; document.hidden still pauses the poll. No backend/endpoint change, no new poll. test_repo_invariants::test_vitals_poll_backs_off_when_panel_closed (cadence exists + chip-only slower than open; self-schedules; the fixed-2s hammer is gone; the network _adaptivePoll is untouched). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 152 invariants green, node --check clean. Browser click-through unverified per fork-3.

item: favicon 404 fixed (brand SVG served + root /favicon.ico route)
summary: Field diagnostics 2026-07-01 (debug bundle: /favicon.ico 404 on every page). ROOT CAUSE: index.html declared no <link rel=icon> and static is mounted only at /static, so the browser's default root /favicon.ico request hit no route -> 404; taskmanager.html pointed at a non-existent /static/favicon.ico. Kills the 404 on every page incl. bare fetches; modern browsers use the crisp SVG directly. No new dep, no schema change, no chrome strings. test_repo_invariants::test_favicon_route_and_brand_icon_wired (asset byte-equals the brand SVG; route registered + resolves to it; /favicon stays allowed-while-locked; both HTML pages declare it and no longer reference favicon.ico). item: IPv6-malformed-URL no longer aborts an article's link extraction
summary: Field diagnostics 2026-07-01 (debug bundle: pipeline WARNING 'link indexing on ingest failed'). ROOT CAUSE: a scraped anchor with an invalid IPv6 literal (href 'http://[::1') makes urlparse raise ValueError 'Invalid IPv6 URL' at the extractor's unguarded netloc/port parse -- the exception aborted the WHOLE article's link-extraction loop, then the regex fallback re-raised it uncaught, so link indexing silently produced nothing for that article. FIX: (a) the main per-element loop wraps _extract_link_from_element in try/except (a single bad href is logged at debug and skipped, the other links preserved); (b) the regex-fallback call site is guarded so a malformed URL degrades to an empty list, never a crash. One bad URL never loses the article's good links. tests/test_link_extractor.py::test_malformed_ipv6_url_does_not_abort_extraction (good + bad-IPv6 + good anchor mix must not raise; both good links kept). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 8 link tests green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline.

item: Ollama BINARY installer in Settings -> AI (download + verify + run the official installer)
key_paths: src/llm/installer.py src/api/llm.py src/static/index.html src/static/app.js tests/test_ollama_installer.py tests/test_repo_invariants.py
summary: The long-unbuilt half of model management (maintainer field test 2026-06-20 'can't find the AI installer'; maintainer Q7=B 2026-06-16). The blocker was always 'we can't fabricate per-OS installer checksums.' RESOLUTION (no fabrication): the GitHub releases API attests a SHA-256 `digest` per release asset -- src/llm/installer.py fetches the ollama/ollama LATEST release through the guarded factory, finds the official install.sh asset + its attested digest, downloads the script, and verifies the bytes against that attestation (resolve_and_verify); a mismatch OR a release that attests no sha256 REFUSES to run (degrade loudly, never run unverified code). Kill-switch gated (no socket under airplane); the script's own later binary download egresses over CLEARNET via curl (Q9, disclosed at consent). ELEVATION EXPLICIT: the verified script is staged under data_dir()/runtime (run_installer refuses anything outside it) and run ONLY when elevation needs no password (root or passwordless sudo -n, so the TTY-less backend can never hang), else the UI shows the exact verified `sudo sh <path>` command for the user's terminal. macOS/Windows: an honest pointer to ollama.com/download (their graphical installers are out of scope -- Debian is the V0.1 target). Endpoints GET /api/llm/install/status + POST /install/prepare + /install/run (NDJSON stream). Frontend: a #llm-install-box panel in Settings -> AI shown only when Ollama is absent -> consent (clearnet disclosure) -> Prepare (shows verified version + sha) -> Install now / verified command -> Recheck. tests/test_ollama_installer.py (12: verify accepts match, refuses mismatch / missing attestation / absent asset; prepare stages + airplane-refusal + unsupported-OS; run refuses outside staging / wrong name / no-elevation; root-run reports exit) + test_repo_invariants::test_ollama_binary_installer. VERIFIED here py3.13: 12 installer + 199 invariants/llm green, ruff F/B clean on the new module, i18n 100% (English-fallback via t(), no new locale keys), routes compose, TestClient status 200. Frontend BROWSER-UNVERIFIED per fork-3. EMPIRICAL FACT: GitHub release assets carry an attested `digest: sha256:...` field -- verify a downloaded installer against it instead of hardcoding/fabricating a checksum. REMAINING: human click-through; key the panel strings x12; an optional task-manager job over a long install.

item: Airplane button: instant feedback + 'Entering airplane mode' pop-up (fix the click lag)
summary: Maintainer field test 2026-06-27: clicking the airplane button lagged a few seconds. ROOT CAUSE: toggleNetwork's OFFLINE branch awaited the POST /api/system/network {online:false} -- which trips the kill switch + installs the process-wide airplane socket guard + stops the scheduler (can take seconds, esp. with an in-flight fetch) -- BEFORE any visual feedback, so the button looked frozen. FIX (the maintainer's suggested option): going offline now reacts INSTANTLY and optimistically -- _paintNetwork(false) flips the airplane glyph FILL, _flashNet(false) fires the direction-aware go-off flash, and a new _airplanePopup() shows a brief centered 'Entering airplane mode' pop-up (auto-dismiss ~1.6s; click the backdrop or OK to close now) -- THEN the POST runs in the BACKGROUND; on success it reconciles with r.online, on failure it reverts the button to online (honest: a refused POST means we are NOT actually offline). The ONLINE path is unchanged (its ensureOnline consent dialog is already the immediate feedback; invariant #14 intact). Extracted the inline flash into _flashNet(online) (reused by both paths; the asserted classList.add(online?'go-on':'go-off') string preserved). New strings 'Entering airplane mode' + 'OK' are English-fallback via t() (NO new locale keys -> zero conflict with the parallel sessions' hot locale files; keyable later). app.css gains a themed .net-popup (uses var(--panel)/--border/--accent so all 17 themes work). test_repo_invariants::test_airplane_toggle_gives_instant_feedback asserts the pop-up + that the paint/pop-up fire BEFORE the POST + the revert; the existing #14 glyph-FILL / ensureOnline-consent / direction-aware-flash / coach assertions all still pass. item: P3 in-app endpoint (follow-up): GET /api/diagnostics/ir-eval over a gold-set file
summary: Keyword-engine P3 follow-up -- the in-app surface that closes the measure-before-trust loop end to end. GET /api/diagnostics/ir-eval?gold_path=&weights_a=&weights_b=&k= loads a SERVER-SIDE gold-set file (ir_eval.load_gold_set) and either scores the live search at the current BM25F default (evaluate_against_corpus) or A/Bs two (title,body) weight sets (bm25f_weight_ab -> conflation_delta, recall/precision/ndcg reported SEPARATELY, no blended score). 400 on a missing/malformed gold set OR half-specified weights (both-or-neither required), via GoldSetError/ValueError -> HTTPException -- never a silent skip. Mirrors the existing diagnostics endpoints (keyword-engine / keyword-selftest / ir-eval-selftest): a GET with download=1 returning a dated attachment. The server-side path input is the established local-single-user pattern (folder-backup dest path / dump reader). So the loop is now COMPLETE in-app: template (configs/ir_eval/gold_set.example.json) -> loader -> endpoint -> report; only the maintainer's GRADED gold-set DATA remains outstanding (corpus-specific, can't be bundled). Not a task-manager job (a bounded read-only eval). tests/test_ir_eval_goldset.py (+1: endpoint single-eval returns oo-ir-eval-1 + the BM25F A/B gives a negative title-heavy->body-heavy ndcg_delta + 400 on a missing file + 400 on half-specified weights) + the test_ir_eval_harness_is_wired invariant extended (the /ir-eval endpoint wires load_gold_set + bm25f_weight_ab). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 150 goldset/invariants + 5 diagnostics tests green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline. REMAINING: a Diagnostics-panel button + path input (deferred, browser-unverified -- curl-able now like the other diagnostics; nothing to run until a gold set exists).

item: P3 operational path (follow-up): gold-set file loader + one-call BM25F A/B + bundled template
summary: Keyword-engine P3 follow-up -- the gold-set INPUT path the harness lacked. P3 had the native metrics + the GoldQuery format + evaluate_against_corpus, but NO documented FILE input and no one-call A/B, so the maintainer had no way to feed graded queries in. Added: (a) ir_eval.load_gold_set(path) parses a JSON gold set ({queries:[{id,query,language,axis,relevances:{docid:0|1|2}}]}) into [GoldQuery], stringifies doc-id keys so they compare regardless of how search returns ids, and FAILS LOUDLY (GoldSetError) on a missing file / bad JSON / out-of-range grade / duplicate id / empty set -- never a silent skip. (b) ir_eval.bm25f_weight_ab(session, gold, weights_a, weights_b) is the one-call A/B of two BM25F (title,body) weight sets over the LIVE corpus + a gold set, returning each side's full report + conflation_delta (recall/precision/ndcg reported SEPARATELY, no blended score). (c) search_ids(..., weights=(wt,wb)) gains a per-call THREAD-SAFE weights override (None = the env default, byte-identical), so the A/B never mutates the process-wide env in the multi-threaded app. (d) a bundled configs/ir_eval/gold_set.example.json TEMPLATE (documented format + 0/1/2 grading guidance + pooled-single-assessor note) the maintainer copies. CORRECTION recorded: an earlier offer to A/B LEMMATIZATION via P3 was incoherent -- lemmatization is a display-layer families change, not a retrieval change, so it is invisible to the FTS retrieval harness; BM25F (P5.1a) IS a retrieval change and is the coherent A/B. tests/test_ir_eval_goldset.py (loader parses the bundled template + 6 malformed-input failure modes; the A/B measures a real title-vs-body ranking move on a fixture FTS corpus -- nDCG moves, recall unchanged, no score) + the test_ir_eval_harness_is_wired invariant extended (loader + A/B + weights override + the template file exist). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 14 goldset/ir-eval/bm25f + 146 invariants + 23 search-surface tests green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline, JSON valid. REMAINING (operational, maintainer): a real graded gold set over the live corpus + run the A/B to pick the BM25F default on evidence; an optional in-app endpoint to run it from a server-side gold-set path (deferred; the library + template ship now).

item: P4.3 measurability (follow-up): lemma_preview in the engine report + a selftest golden case
summary: Keyword-engine P4.3 follow-up -- the measure-before-trust INSTRUMENTS so the maintainer can review + regression-guard the OPT-IN lemmatization WITHOUT enabling it blindly. (a) engine_report.py gains a lemma_preview block = the candidate conflations among the top-N single-token TERMS (groups that share a lemma per language, e.g. study/studied), with the would-merge member sets + counts, so the maintainer eyeballs PRECISION before flipping OO_FAMILY_LEMMA (a wrong merge -> a _MISLEMMA_DENYLIST entry). Reports available:false honestly when the optional simplemma is absent; mirrors the families guards (single-token terms only, never entity NAMES); reuses the existing top-N keyword scan (adds Keyword.language + is_entity to it); no score. (b) selftest.py gains a lemmatization_mechanism golden case that checks _lemma() DIRECTLY (no env toggle -> thread-safe in the live multi-threaded process; not the global build_families gate) -- study/studied -> study + running -> run + Wahlen -> wahl, and the denylist blocks media !-> medium -- so a conflation regression reddens BOTH the in-app keyword-selftest export the maintainer sends AND CI; omitted on a core install (simplemma absent), never a failure. Surfaced via the existing GET /api/diagnostics/keyword-engine + /keyword-selftest (no new endpoint). tests/test_keyword_engine_report.py (lemma_preview surfaces study/studied, denylist excluded, no score, degrades) + tests/test_keyword_selftest.py (the mechanism case present + passes when simplemma available, omitted otherwise). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 41 keyword + 30 diagnostic tests green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline.

item: P4.3 simplemma lemmatization at the families display layer (OPT-IN, default off)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization P4.3 (mechanism). src/analytics/families.py gains a lemma-collapse grouping step (1.6) that conflates morphological keyword variants a plural heuristic MISSES -- verb forms + irregulars (study/studied -> study, run/running -> run, child/children -> child, Wahlen -> Wahl) -- via simplemma (pure-Python, NO torch/network; added to the [analysis] extra). _lemma(norm, lang) is CONSERVATIVE: single-token TERMS only (never entity NAMES), per (kind, LANGUAGE) so an en term never merges a fr one, a _MISLEMMA_DENYLIST blocks meaning-changers (media->medium, data->datum, us->we -- evidence-grown like _PLURAL_DENYLIST), and unsupported languages (zh/ja + ones simplemma covers poorly) + a missing simplemma + any lemmatizer error all FALL BACK to norm (graceful degrade -- a core install is a no-op). HONESTY CALLS: (a) DISPLAY layer ONLY -- families.py, NEVER _normalize / the stored trusted index (rewriting the canonical index is forbidden); an invariant asserts extract.py/store.py never import simplemma. (b) REVERSIBLE -- a user split override keeps a form out. (c) VISIBLE conflated_by=['lemma'] provenance on the family (exposed in to_dict). (d) DEFAULT OFF (OO_FAMILY_LEMMA, default '0') -- the measure-before-trust discipline: it changes grouping app-wide, so its retrieval-quality impact MUST be measured (the P3 eval harness + a human-judged gold set) before it is trusted on-by-default; default-off + the skip => BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-lemma grouping (the plural rule still handles regular -s/-es/-ies). tests/test_families.py (+5, skip-guarded on simplemma: lemma unit + guards; verb/irregular collapse + conflated_by; off-by-default no-merge [runs everywhere]; entity/denylist/reversible; graceful-degrade without simplemma) + test_repo_invariants::test_lemmatization_is_opt_in_display_layer_and_reversible. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 160 families/invariants + 19 build_families-consumers green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline, pyproject valid, external-freshness guard green. REMAINING: (1) the maintainer ENABLES + MEASURES it via P3 on a gold set before it goes default-on (operational); (2) the frontend display of conflated_by (deferred, browser-unverified -- nothing renders while the feature is off).

NO new endpoint duplicates the surface: the existing When/Where/Who subtab is upgraded IN PLACE (no parallel-window debt). Frontend: the #an-www loader renders who/where/when as clickable .an-facet chips (count shown) -> branchByFacet -> drill -> openAnalysisForIds; _anFacets state; honest empty states + visible caveat; new strings English-fallback via t() (i18n gate stays 100%). item: P5.1a BM25F per-column ranking weights (title vs body)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization P5.1 first half (the second half -- entity/temporal/geographic facets -- is a distinct larger slice, P5.1b). src/database/fts.py search_ids now ranks ORDER BY bm25(article_fts, :wt, :wb) instead of the flat 'rank', weighting the TITLE column above the BODY: a title keyword is a stronger relevance signal than a body mention. Verified empirically that FTS5 bm25 column weights do this -- bm25(ft,10,1) ranks a title-only match above a body-only match, bm25(ft,1,10) flips it. _bm25_weights() reads OO_BM25_TITLE_WEIGHT (default 4.0) / OO_BM25_BODY_WEIGHT (default 1.0), clamps >=0, and FALLS BACK to the default on a bad value (never crashes). The weights are BOUND PARAMETERS (:wt/:wb), never f-string-formatted into SQL (no bandit B608 surface). REVERSIBLE by construction -- equal weights == the old flat rank. ONE change covers every consumer: search_ids is the single FTS ranking entry point (omnibar, _query_articles, framing, reporting, watches, AND the P3 evaluate_against_corpus), so the P3 harness can A/B a weight set via conflation_delta the moment a gold set exists -- the measure-before-trust loop is now closed for ranking. tests/test_bm25f.py (3: a title-only vs body-only match -> title ranks first; env reversal -> body ranks first; default wt>wb + bad-env fallback) + test_repo_invariants guard (BM25F wiring, weights bound not string-formatted). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 21 search-suite + 18 ir-eval/watches + 144 invariants green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline, py_compile.

item: P3 IR retrieval-eval harness (the quality-measurement gate)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization Phase 3 -- the retrieval-eval gate (project rule: measure before you trust; a prerequisite for the ranking/conflation changes P5.1 BM25F, P4.3 lemmatization, P5.2 embeddings). src/analytics/ir_eval.py: NATIVE pure-Python IR metrics (nDCG@k/MRR@k/Recall@k/P@k/AP, textbook defs unit-tested vs hand-computed values -- NO new [eval] dependency to gate/degrade; the strategy allowed pytrec_eval/ranx OR native, native is leaner + more reliable). GoldQuery (id/query/language/axis/graded relevances 0-1-2). evaluate() reports PER-LANGUAGE + per-axis with n stated, NEVER one pooled average alone (a method can win overall while losing on one language), and NO composite score (each metric stands alone). conflation_delta() reports the recall GAIN and precision CHANGE SEPARATELY + the newly-relevant vs newly-irrelevant example sets (never blended). regression_check() fails on a metric drop beyond tol. evaluate_against_corpus() runs the live FTS search_ids (pluggable search_fn so a BM25F/hybrid variant A/Bs via conflation_delta). run_ir_eval_selftest() proves the MECHANISM on a fixture (10/10) -> GET /api/diagnostics/ir-eval-selftest. tests/test_ir_eval.py (8: metrics vs hand-computed, per-language breakdown + no-composite, conflation both-sides, regression gate, injected search_fn) + invariant guard. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 155 targeted green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline. OPERATIONAL remainder: a human-judged GOLD SET (graded 0/1/2 over the maintainer's own corpus) -- corpus-specific, can't be bundled; the harness consumes it.

item: P4.2 reconcile_keyword_language (signature-majority re-language)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization P4.2. reconcile_keyword_language(session) sets Keyword.language to the SIGNATURE-MAJORITY article language -- the fix for the first-write-wins tag index_article never reconciles (the 16%/40%-of-head mismatch that gates correct grouping + later lemmatization). Background pass mirroring reconcile_keyword_counters: flips only on a CLEAR majority (>half of the keyword's located mentions) backed by >=2 distinct articles, so a stray article never flips a tag. PERF-SAFE (the SQLCipher codec column-order trap): NEVER the per-row keyword_mentions->articles join (drags ~35KB rows); instead a covering article-language map (idx_article_language, no content read) + a covering (keyword_id,article_id) mention scan joined in Python (one mention per (kw,article) so a row count == distinct articles). Endpoint POST /api/insights/reconcile-keyword-language; folded into the re-index job's complete pass (Clean-up-keywords now fixes language too). The '?' bucket (all-untagged mentions) is left as-is: global_stopwords ALREADY routes every keyword incl. unknown-language through the English+all-language stoplist at query time, so '?' boilerplate is filtered there; an aggressive email/web boilerplate denylist stays the evidence-driven stoplist process (analyze_keyword_log.py), never a guess (no-over-stoplist discipline). tests/test_analytics_store.py (signature-majority flip + NULL->lang + tie-no-flip + ?-noop) + invariant guard. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 193 targeted green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline.

item: P2.4 DuckDB-1.4 GCM verify (the persisted-columnar D1 unblock)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization P2.4 (VERIFY-FIRST). Installed DuckDB 1.5.4 in the venv and empirically tested whether >=1.4 writes an authenticated AES-256-GCM encrypted store NATIVELY (without the OpenSSL httpfs extension), which would let us relax secure_crypto_available and PERSIST the rollups (the real perf win). REFUTED: DuckDB 1.5.4 REFUSES an encrypted WRITE without LOAD httpfs -- error 'DuckDB currently has a read-only crypto module loaded ... ensure httpfs is loaded ... To write an encrypted database ... that is NOT securely encrypted, one can use SET force_mbedtls_unsafe=true.' The only no-httpfs write path is the explicitly-UNSAFE mbedtls = the forbidden fabricated-security. VERDICT: do NOT relax the gate; keep the engine IN-MEMORY; the persisted-rollup perf win stays blocked on bundling per-OS httpfs binaries (OPERATIONAL, networked machine -- never fabricate). 2nd finding: 1.5.x enable_external_access=False also blocks a file ATTACH outright (moot while httpfs gates). Recorded in columnar.py EMPIRICAL FINDING. No code change (the gate already returns False = correct).

item: P1.4 FTS5 'optimize' + planner tuning pass after a bulk load (Phase 1 COMPLETE)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization Phase 1.4 (the SQLCipher+FTS5 tuning pass; completes Phase 1). src/database/fts.py:optimize_after_bulk(session) runs the FTS5 'optimize' segment-merge (INSERT INTO article_fts(article_fts) VALUES('optimize') -- DISTINCT from PRAGMA optimize; verified it did NOT run before, only 'rebuild' at init) + PRAGMA optimize (planner stats, analysis_limit-bounded). Both gated (writes) + SQLite-only + best-effort (a tuning failure incl. a missing FTS table never breaks the caller). Wired after a COMPLETE re-index pass (keyword-table churn -> planner) AND after the newsletter folder import (article bulk-load -> FTS segment churn). cache_size left at the memory-conservative OO_SQLITE_CACHE_MB default 64 MiB for the reference AppVM (mmap unavailable under the codec so cache_size is the lever -- documented); no default change. tests/test_fts_optimize.py (the merge keeps FTS search exact; best-effort without an FTS table) + the invariant guard. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 167 targeted green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline. PHASE 1 (unblock-the-rebuild) COMPLETE: 1.1 job + 1.2 keyword-only + 1.3 batched commits + 1.4 tuning.

item: P1.3 batched commits in the re-index (COLLECTOR_WRITER_BATCHING)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization Phase 1.3 (the data-loss-critical writer path; keystone #1). index_article gains commit:bool=True (default byte-identical; False leaves mentions+counter-deltas+when/where/who PENDING so a caller batches several articles into ONE commit/fsync). reindex_all_batch gains commit_batch:int=1 (default per-article=byte-identical; >1 commits every N) with the PROVEN ingest_emails rollback-then-redo-per-article fallback (_redo_committed): a batch-commit failure (transient lock) OR an error building one article rolls the batch back and redoes it one-at-a-time committed, a bad/locked one isolated -- idempotent re-index reproduces the full result, so NO batch-mate is ever dropped. The ReindexJobManager reads OO_REINDEX_COMMIT_BATCH (default 1). NO-LOSS tests: batched==per-article with counters==the live GROUP BY (zero drift), failure-fallback loses nothing, AND a real-SessionLocal contention test (a batched re-index HOLDING the gate across a batch races concurrent ingest -> zero locks, zero loss, exact sentinel counters, no gate leak). DECISION (autonomous, ledgered): the COLLECTOR-path batching (the doc's store_fetched restructure) is DEFERRED -- live-perf-gated per its own doc (unmeasurable in-session) + the riskiest 50-worker hot-path change; the commit=False primitive now exists so it is a smaller follow-up when the maintainer can measure live. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 57-test ingest/index hot-path regression + 160 targeted green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline.

item: P1.2 keyword-only re-index scope
summary: Keyword-engine optimization Phase 1.2. index_article gains scope=full|keywords: keywords recomputes the keyword pass ONLY (skips when/where/who [dates/places/entities] + sentiment, ~2/3 less work for a keyword cleanup); the language deduction stays in both (it picks the extraction stoplist). Threaded through reindex_all_batch -> the ReindexJobManager (persisted + reported in status) -> POST /api/insights/reindex-job?scope= (400 on a bad scope). The Settings Clean-up-keywords button now uses keyword-only; Re-index-the-whole-corpus stays full. Default scope=full is byte-identical (no ingest hot-path change). Tests: test_analytics_store (keyword-only skips when/where/who+sentiment via the tally + leaves sentiment untouched; identical keyword rows to a full pass) + test_reindex_job (scope plumbs through the job) + the invariant scope guard. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 47-test ingest/index hot-path regression + 153 targeted green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline, node --check, i18n 100%. Frontend browser-unverified (fork-3).

item: P1.1 backend re-index JOB (persisted cursor, pausable, task-manager-visible)
summary: Keyword-engine optimization Phase 1.1. Moves the whole-corpus re-index OFF the client loop (which restarted from article 0 and stopped on tab close) into a backend JOB mirroring NewsletterImportManager: worker thread + stop-event PAUSE + on-disk persisted CURSOR (data_dir()/reindex_job.json = last article id + total/done/tally/prune_after), so it survives a tab close/app restart and RESUMES from where it stopped. Drives reindex_all_batch; DB-writer kind=reindex joins the collect/import/reindex single-writer arbitration; idempotent re-index is the no-loss net. Endpoints POST /api/insights/reindex-job + /reindex-job/{action} + GET /reindex-job/status; surfaced in /api/jobs (_reindex_jobs, pause/resume routed); the Settings Clean-up + Re-index buttons now start+poll the job (legacy _reindexAllLoop/_pruneCore kept defined as fallback + for the invariant test). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 6 job tests + test_reindex_background_job_is_wired + jobs/insights regression green, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline, node --check, i18n 100%, audit-chrome clean. Frontend browser-unverified (fork-3).

status: shipped (backend verified py3.11; full 3.13 suite in CI)

summary: Live 727k-keyword corpus still leaked grammar in de/ru/es/it/pt/nl and other MANAGED languages (they were claimed-managed via PARTIAL hand-grown _EXTRA_STOPWORD_TEXT batches; get_stopwords('de') fell through to the ENGLISH default). Vendored the FULL stopwords-iso lists (ar bg da de el es hr hu id it nl no nb pl pt ru sl sv) into configs/stopwords_iso/. These load into scoped_stopwords, applied LANGUAGE-SCOPED at extraction and kept OUT of the language-agnostic global_stopwords() union -- that scoping makes a full Latin-script list (de 620, es 732) collision-free: verified English keeps man/state/union though they are in the German list. Closed-class only (articles/pronouns/prepositions/conjunctions/auxiliaries); open-class content (lexical verbs, adjectives, common nouns) deliberately KEPT (dual-use, no POS tagger); log-proven boilerplate still pruned per-language. sr/bs/az keep hand-grown batches (absent from iso); nb aliased to the Bokmaal-based no list. Managed SET unchanged -- only stoplist QUALITY improves; existing keywords clear on re-index. STOPWORDS_ISO_AS_OF stays 2026-06 (same snapshot); registry last_verified -> 2026-07-01. EMPIRICAL FACT/LESSON: language-scoped stoplists (scoped_stopwords) are collision-free vs the global union, so a full list can be vendored per language without the length>=4/accented-only constraint the global _EXTRA_STOPWORDS batches need. tests/test_stopwords_iso.py +2 guards; test_external_freshness green.

item: Open-class garbage detector + closed-class/platform batch
summary: Open-class (adjective/noun) garbage. Grounded on the real 727k-keyword log filtered through the CURRENT stoplist (function-word garbage already fixed by #525/#528). CORE = analyze_keyword_log.py --generic-terms: surfaces high document-frequency single-word TERMS surviving the stoplist (the generic words no function list catches), ranked by article-spread + df_ratio, excluding entities/acronyms/proper-noun-suspects/rings/stoplisted; EVERY row a REVIEW candidate (ubiquity flags generic boilerplate AND a dominant topic identically, no POS tagger) - a microscope, never auto-applied; parse_stoplist now ingests the vendored *.txt too. SAFE WINS applied (low dual-use, detector-exposed): English indefinite pronouns (something/everyone/nothing/none... - closed-class gap, global-safe) + platform FURNITURE English-precedented (photo/video/story) = podcast/newsletter/gallery/cookies/comments/advertisement (global) + language-SCOPED per-lang (de inhalte/werbung, es publicidad, it/pt/nl/ru); 'content' kept OUT of global (fr=happy collision). MEASURED: newly hides 43 keyword rows / 20,747 mentions on the real log. NOT swept (dual-use proven by data): topic adjectives/nouns (health/policy/system/salud) stay content -> detector review; inflected generic verbs (zeigen/voir) = lemmatization/held Lever gated on the eval harness. LESSON: open-class garbage has no safe blanket rule (dual-use + no POS); the honest levers are corpus-statistical detection (df ubiquity) + a tight English-precedented platform/closed-class batch, NOT a category sweep. selftest control word newsletter->layout. Tests +3; ruff clean; mypy src delta 0.

item: keyword_daily rollup (D2): streamed build + windowed serve + in-memory parity
summary: D2 of the 1000x derived-layer scaling (docs/design/SCALING_DERIVED_LAYER_1000X.md), the deep fix for the measured Insights freeze (windowed top_terms/trending = 17s scanning the 2.4M-row keyword_mentions table, each in-range row a SQLCipher page decrypt). ADDS to columnar.py: build_keyword_daily (the streamed FULL build - column-projected batches out of SQLite into a DuckDB staging table, GROUP BY THERE into per-(keyword,day) mentions=SUM(count) + articles_on_day=COUNT(DISTINCT article_id); records last_mention_id for D3 incremental) + a keyword_meta projection + windowed_term_counts / windowed_top_terms_raw serve primitives (reproduce the live windowed aggregation from the rollup; kind filter reproduces _apply_kind) + a keyword_daily_parity probe. DORMANT by design: NOT wired to the hot read path here - serving safely needs the corpus-epoch guard + epoch-bump-on-mutate (D3) so a re-index can never make an incremental rollup double-count; this slice is the builder+serve+parity scaffold, ZERO runtime-behaviour change, proven in-memory as the design mandates. HONESTY: mentions (SUM) is EXACT; the distinct-article count is disclosed as an UPPER BOUND (summing per-day distincts double-counts a keyword-article pair seen on multiple days). EMPIRICAL/LESSON: the unique (keyword_id,article_id) index means each pair has exactly one row on one day, so the bound is in fact EXACT today (gap 0, parity-tested) - we still disclose the upper bound because the rollup STRUCTURE cannot guarantee exactness on its own (a future per-occurrence-with-date mention schema would make the gap real); disclose what the structure can prove, not the value it happens to yield. tests/test_keyword_daily_rollup.py (7: full-history exact parity, windowed parity + ranking, batch-size invariance, upper-bound structure + gap reported, cold-fallback, kind filter, parity probe). ruff F/B clean; 43 existing columnar/data-arch tests green; full pytest needs py3.13 -> CI.

item: keyword_daily incremental refresh + corpus-epoch guard (D3 core)
summary: D3 (correctness-critical core) of the 1000x derived-layer scaling. refresh_keyword_daily(con, session, corpus_epoch): FULL rebuild when the corpus epoch changed since the last build (a re-index/prune/restore happened) OR no usable prior build, else an INCREMENTAL merge of just the new mention tail (id > last_mention_id watermark) via a portable DuckDB MERGE (UPDATE ... FROM + INSERT ... WHERE NOT EXISTS; DuckDB 1.5.4 verified). New keywords first seen in a tail get their keyword_meta upserted (else the JOIN-based serve would drop them). THE TRAP (grounded): index_article does delete-then-reinsert, so an id-watermark merge-ADD is correct ONLY for APPEND - a re-index leaves the OLD contribution in the rollup AND re-inserts higher-id rows into the tail = a doubled number; so re-index/prune/restore mutators bump a CORPUS EPOCH and a changed epoch forces a FULL rebuild, never incremental. The epoch lives on the CANONICAL side and is PASSED IN here - this slice owns only the refresh decision + the merge, dormant at runtime, proven in-memory as the design mandates. DEFERRED to the next slice: wiring the canonical epoch counter into the mutators + wiring the serve into the hot read path behind the built_epoch==corpus_epoch guard + the basis flag. tests/test_keyword_daily_refresh.py (6, VERIFY 4/5/6): incremental==full-rebuild on append, late-arriving historical-dated batch lands on the correct old day, epoch-change forces full AND an UNGUARDED incremental after a re-index really does double-count (proves why the guard exists), no-op when nothing new, new-keyword-in-tail gets metadata, missing-rollup->full. LESSON: the delete-then-reinsert pattern makes an id-watermark incremental rollup double-count across ANY re-index/prune; guard every derived-append rollup with an epoch bumped by exactly the non-append mutators. ruff F/B clean; 34 existing columnar/data-arch tests green; full pytest needs py3.13 -> CI.

item: Opt-in in-memory rollup serve for windowed top_terms (OO_COLUMNAR_SERVE)
summary: The real windowed speedup a long-running app can USE tonight WITHOUT the D1 persisted store (which needs per-OS crypto binaries bundled). src/analytics/rollup_serve.py holds ONE process-lifetime in-memory DuckDB keyword_daily rollup, builds it ONCE in a BACKGROUND thread, and serves windowed most-mentioned rows from it instead of scanning the multi-GB mentions table (the Insights/trends freeze). OFF BY DEFAULT - only OO_COLUMNAR_SERVE=1 (explicit opt-in, never a silent default). SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION: every serve wrapped -> ANY problem returns None and top_terms falls back to the live query (identical results); build is background (never blocks a request); the shared DuckDB con is lock-guarded (not thread-safe) and the build works on its OWN con + swaps atomically (a serve never touches a half-built store); FULL rebuild (always correct, no incremental double-count trap) refreshed periodically (OO_COLUMNAR_SERVE_TTL_S, 15min) + after each scrape via warm_cache. queries.top_terms windowed path: tries rollup_serve.windowed_rows (time-window only, never per-country - the rollup has no country dim; never the corpus-wide counter path), else live; both feed the SAME hidden-word/family/ring/translation tail so output is byte-compatible; attaches a basis disclosure (source=columnar-rollup + as_of + the upper-bound note) when served. Numbers identical to live today (mentions exact; distinct exact under the unique keyword+article index); the only real difference is bounded staleness (as-of last build), disclosed in basis. tests/test_rollup_serve.py (5): off-by-default=live-untouched, opted-in-not-built=fallback, opted-in-built=same values + basis, per-country=live, corpus-wide=untouched. REMAINING: wire trending_windows (Home trending, the most-visible freeze) the same way; render the basis disclosure visibly in the UI; the persisted-store durability (survive restart, no per-process rebuild) is D1 (per-OS httpfs packaging). ruff F/B + bandit clean; 51 targeted tests green; full pytest py3.13->CI.

item: Legacy restore preview/commit off the event loop (stops the Previewing freeze)
summary: field report: a small backup stuck on 'Previewing - nothing is changed yet' for an hour. ROOT CAUSE: restore_preview/restore_commit were async def handlers doing the heavy work SYNCHRONOUSLY (decrypt+unzip, copy the WHOLE live corpus to working.db, run the FULL dry-run merge - the can't-lie guarantee makes preview ~as costly as a commit) ON the event loop, freezing the single-worker server for the whole restore (task manager wouldn't load, polls stalled, UI hung). FIX: move the blocking stage+merge into run_in_threadpool (extracted _preview_sync/_commit_sync) so the server stays responsive; honest preview message (copies+dry-run-merges the corpus, minutes on a large corpus, app stays usable in another tab). No merge logic changed. LESSON: a FastAPI async def handler runs ON the event loop (only def handlers get the threadpool) - heavy synchronous work in one freezes the whole single-worker server; thread it.

Keyed by corpus epoch (a re-index/prune/restore forces a rebuild) AND the article/mention id watermarks (ordinary ingest picked up); FULL rebuild only (small result, no incremental to get wrong). Read-only/in-memory/airplane-safe. 5 tests (parity vs source_country_counts, honest mean-tone, unlocated bucket, epoch/watermark refresh decision, benchmark shape); 0 new mypy errors; bandit clean. item: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR module (the multiple-testing spine)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13)
summary: Pure, textbook BH (1995) false-discovery-rate control -- the shared multiple-testing correction any SCREENING analytic (lunar correlation, flood/bury, manipulation scans) runs before surfacing a result, so a screen of m tests can honestly report 'these survived correction' instead of the ~alpha*m false positives a bare significant p-value produces. benjamini_hochberg(pvalues,q,dependency) -> FdrResult{q,m,rejected(original indices),n_rejected,threshold(BH cutoff or None),adjusted(q-values in input order),dependency,method}; bh_adjusted() = the step-up q-values. No dependency, no I/O, no state. Standard BH (independent/positive dependence) + the conservative BH-Yekutieli variant for arbitrary dependence. A DISCLOSURE (which survive at level q + by how much), never a score. Validated two ways: hand-computed cutoffs on textbook datasets (the m=8 case rejects {0,1} at thr 0.011; the classic 15-hypothesis dataset rejects 1..4) AND cross-checked against scipy.stats.false_discovery_control over 20 random cases (scipy is a dev/test dep only, never a runtime dependency). 9 tests; 0 new mypy errors; ruff clean.

item: Flood/bury card #4 — the BURY half (source under-covering a corpus-wide topic, FDR-corrected)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; auto-renders via the generic Home-card path)
summary: The inverse of the shipped flood detector (which used a source's own temporal baseline). find_buried_topics: for every (candidate source, big topic) pair a two-proportion z-test compares the SOURCE's share of the topic vs the REST-OF-CORPUS share (one-sided, is the source BELOW?). The corpus itself is the 'big elsewhere' (a real internal trigger, resolving the maintainer note that the bury half needed one). The whole family of pairs is corrected with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR (src/stats/fdr, item 3) so screening thousands of pairs cannot manufacture a finding; a pair surfaces only if it BOTH survives FDR AND clears z<=-z_min. A topic is 'big' by distinct SOURCES (breadth, the independence measure) + distinct articles + corpus share. item: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR module (the multiple-testing spine)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13)
summary: Pure, textbook BH (1995) false-discovery-rate control -- the shared multiple-testing correction any SCREENING analytic (lunar correlation, flood/bury, manipulation scans) runs before surfacing a result, so a screen of m tests can honestly report 'these survived correction' instead of the ~alpha*m false positives a bare significant p-value produces. benjamini_hochberg(pvalues,q,dependency) -> FdrResult{q,m,rejected(original indices),n_rejected,threshold(BH cutoff or None),adjusted(q-values in input order),dependency,method}; bh_adjusted() = the step-up q-values. No dependency, no I/O, no state. Standard BH (independent/positive dependence) + the conservative BH-Yekutieli variant for arbitrary dependence. A DISCLOSURE (which survive at level q + by how much), never a score. Validated two ways: hand-computed cutoffs on textbook datasets (the m=8 case rejects {0,1} at thr 0.011; the classic 15-hypothesis dataset rejects 1..4) AND cross-checked against scipy.stats.false_discovery_control over 20 random cases (scipy is a dev/test dep only, never a runtime dependency). 9 tests; 0 new mypy errors; ruff clean.

item: rollup_serve serves ONLY the DB it was built over (cross-DB correctness net)
summary: Regression caught by a full-suite health check after the parallel wave: test_briefing::test_rising_producer_surfaces_trending_term failed only in-suite (passed alone) = cross-test pollution from the process-lifetime rollup_serve singleton, which #567 made AUTO-ON when duckdb is present. ROOT CAUSE: windowed_rows/windowed_counts took a _session but IGNORED it, serving the in-memory keyword_daily rollup (built over the process SessionLocal DB via session_scope) to ANY caller — so a query bound to a DIFFERENT engine (a test fixture's create_engine, or in principle any ad-hoc connection) got another corpus's windowed counts, and trending/rising came up empty. FIX: _build_and_swap records _STATE['bind'] (the engine it built over); a new _same_bind(session, built_bind) gates both serve functions on `session.get_bind() is built_bind`, so a non-matching bind falls back to the live query (the standing fallback-to-live safety, now bind-aware). In production every caller uses get_db -> the process engine == what the rollup was built over, so serving is unchanged; only foreign-DB callers change (they now correctly fall back). test_rollup_serve manual builds updated to record the bind they build over (the honest invariant) + the reset fixture clears it. Verified py3.13: 7 rollup_serve tests green, the 13-a-file + rising pollution repro 76 passed (was 1 failed), OO_COLUMNAR_SERVE=0 confirmed the diagnosis; ruff F/B clean; 0 new mypy errors. LESSON: a process-lifetime in-memory accelerator keyed only on time-staleness will serve one database's numbers to another database's query — key it to the bind/corpus it was built over and fall back to live on any mismatch; auto-on-in-tests is what exposed it (merged != green — the maintainer fast-merges, so a full-suite health check after a parallel wave catches order-dependent pollution the per-PR CI can miss).

item: Orphan settings JSON -> encrypted DB via an app_state kv table
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13)
key_paths: src/config/kv_store.py src/database/models.py migrations/versions/b3d4e5f6a7c8_app_state.py src/config/app_settings.py src/scheduler/settings.py src/custody/settings.py src/safety/settings.py src/backup/merge.py tests/test_app_state_kv.py
summary: DB-reliability D1: the four settings JSON side-files (app/scheduler/custody/safety) were not transactional, not in the encrypted store, and fully orphaned from every backup. Moved them into a key->value row of the corpus DB (AppState model + migration b3d4e5f6a7c8 on the single head e7f8a9b0c1d2; create_all + a kv self-heal also build the table). src/config/kv_store.py is the generic app_state primitive: it resolves the live SQLite file the LAZY (current-data_dir) way through the ONE connect() factory (so per-test OO_DATA_DIR isolation AND SQLCipher both keep working -- the shared engine is import-time-bound and would have broken the per-test settings isolation the suite relies on), takes the process-wide single-writer gate for writes (a settings save can never collide with the collector), and caches hot reads (custody-per-ingest) keyed by db-path. Each settings module keeps its EXACT load_settings/save_settings API: read DB-first, fall back to and one-time-migrate the legacy JSON, write to the DB. app_state joins _MERGE_IGNORED (settings are per-machine; local wins on restore, design T10); the legacy side-file merge path is untouched for old backups. Verified: 12 new tests + existing settings/torture side-file tests green; init_db AND alembic upgrade both create app_state; mypy 127 unchanged; ruff F,B clean. Deferred (frontend, Session F): the Settings hint string 'Stored locally in app_settings.json' is now stale copy.

item: Server-side agenda calendar-subscription prefs over app_state
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend+endpoint verified py3.13; UI is Session F)
summary: DB-reliability D4: agenda smart-calendar subscriptions (which calendars to show/exclude + the view) lived only in browser localStorage, so the server never saw them and they were absent from every backup. src/config/agenda_prefs.py backs them with the app_state kv (key agenda.prefs), shape-validated (list-of-short-strings + a bounded view) with a `configured` flag so an unconfigured server tells the client to keep its first-run default (subscribe to every calendar) rather than silently dropping calendars. New router src/api/agenda_state.py (GET/PUT /api/agenda/prefs), wired in _wiring.py. Backend + endpoint + tests only; the frontend adopts the REST path later (keeps its localStorage fallback until then). 8 new tests green; ruff F,B clean; mypy +0.

item: Full-text search over downloaded Wikipedia dumps
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend+endpoints verified py3.13; UI is Session F)
summary: Downloaded multistream dumps were readable one title at a time but their BODIES were not full-text-searchable. Added a disposable, rebuildable content index kept as a SEPARATE side-file so the encrypted corpus and the watched-page corpus path are literally untouched (and it inherits wiki_dumps/ backup-exclusion). dumpread.iter_pages() is a single O(N) streaming enumerator (visit each bz2 block once), the whole-dump primitive that was missing. src/wiki/dump_index.py builds an FTS5 index at data_dir()/wiki_dumps/dump_index.sqlite (plaintext -- public dump text already sits as plaintext .bz2 beside it), reusing fts.build_match so the Boolean syntax + BM25F ranking match article search; redirects/other namespaces skipped; a background DumpIndexManager runs the long sweep off the request thread. Endpoints in wiki.py: GET /dumps/fts-search, GET/POST/DELETE /dumps/index (+ cancel). Acceptance: a body-only term in a downloaded dump is found by content search, and the article FTS is provably unchanged (test_dump_index_does_not_touch_article_fts). 10 new tests + 32 existing dump/FTS tests green; ruff F,B clean; mypy 127 unchanged. Deferred (search-owning session): fold dump hits into the omnibar _wiki_group.

item: Heavy keyword export runs on a read-only WAL snapshot (field finding C)
summary: Field finding C: the keyword-diagnostics export scans keyword_mentions twice and the field log measured it running SLOWER on a second run because it contended with the live scrape. src/database/read_snapshot.py adds a dedicated read-only engine (NullPool) opened through the ONE connect() factory with PRAGMA query_only=ON (the export can NEVER take the write gate or stall a writer, by construction) and the documented pysqlite recipe (autocommit driver + explicit BEGIN on transaction start) so the whole streamed export holds ONE consistent WAL snapshot -- a racing write can neither corrupt nor stall it, and its two scans agree. read_only_db COMPOSES get_db so it transparently inherits a test dependency override and only swaps to the read engine when it sees the real production engine (no test edits needed; the existing keyword-export tests keep their own engine override, and the production swap-path is exercised end-to-end by test_young_corpus_and_diagnostics). keyword_log now depends on read_only_db. Contention proven in test_read_snapshot (query_only refuses writes; snapshot stays consistent under a concurrent commit; a held snapshot never blocks a writer). mypy 127 unchanged; ruff F,B clean.

item: UI batch F: world-map linear/log time toggle + slope & small-multiples charts + Home enlarge + Source-competitive ported into #an
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; frontend browser-unverified per fork-3)
key_paths: src/static/app.js src/static/ooviz.js src/static/index.html src/static/locales/*.json tests/test_ui_map_time_scale.py tests/test_ui_ooviz_slope_multiples.py tests/test_ui_home_trend_strip.py tests/test_ui_analysis_window_consolidation.py
summary: Frontend-only wave over existing endpoints. ITEM 1: a labelled Linear/Log scale toggle on the ooMap Signals time slider (was hard-coded log); LOG stays the default (antiquity-compressing sweep, unchanged, still invariant-pinned via Math.pow(_LOGB,1-frac)), LINEAR gives an even year-by-year sweep. A tick strip names the actual year at 0/quarter/half/three-quarter/full so the compression is always explicit (honest labelled ticks, no hidden warp). Map-toggle chrome keyed x12 (Time scale/Linear/Log + 2 tooltips, AI-drafted, flagged), matching the rest of the ooMap controls. Pure frontend over /api/timemap. ITEM 2: two new pure ooViz primitives (slopeGeometry + gridLayout, node-verified: shared value scale, gap breaks, deterministic grid) and two renderers (slopeChartSvg, smallMultiplesSvg) wired to /api/insights/trending-windows via a Windows/Rate-slope/Small-multiples lens toggle in the Insights Trends subtab (reuses the already-fetched payload, no extra request; default Windows unchanged). Slope = per-day mention RATE across the nested 24h/week/month windows (count/window-days so magnitudes are comparable; a term absent from a window is a gap, never zero; colour encodes direction, direct-labelled endpoints deep-link). Small-multiples = one shared 0..max vertical scale across all panels (line when dense, bars when sparse per invariant #16, n shown, no interpolated curve, counts only/no score, caveats visible). ITEM 3: the Home 'Trending now' strip already met the dashboard spec (top rising terms' sparklines, deep-links, fail-safe hidden); added the missing click-to-enlarge into the interactive ooChart (reuses chartEnlarge + the stashed payload, no extra fetch), matching the Insights Trends UX. ITEM 4 (two-window consolidation): routing already converges on the #an flagship (openCorpus->openAnalysisFor). The ONE capability the retired #corpus-win modal still had that #an lacked was Source-competitive; ported it INTO #an as an additive facet (nav button + #an-competitive panel + lazy renderAnCompetitive) reusing /api/insights/corpus-sources (scoped to the #an corpus, article_ids OR query) + /api/framing (query-only, honestly absent for an article-set corpus). Descriptive divergence ordered by volume only, no ranking/winner/composite score, VADER English-only tone disclosure, n=1='nothing to compare'. ADDITIVE (the dead modal + its renderCorpusCompetitive are untouched, so the existing corpus-window invariant stays green) -> #an now covers every modal sub-tab, so the modal can be retired in a later browser-verified pass with nothing lost. ITEM 5 (dead temporal-map JS cleanup): DEFERRED with a note, per the ledger's standing deferral (browser-unverifiable + the interleaved-shared-helper hazard: tmapFindCoverage — shared/live, used by ooMap's signal detail — sits literally between the dead tmapNearby and showTmapDetail; kindColor/TMAP_KINDS/fmtYear/fmtDate/dateToT/lon2x/lat2y are also interleaved with the dead functions). A ~250-line interleaved deletion for zero user value, unverifiable without a browser, is exactly the between-session regression the ledger warns against; the World map's feature absorption was already verified at slice 5b, so the dead functions are inert. VERIFIED here (py3.11 sandbox): node --check on app.js+ooviz.js, the ooviz node test (22), i18n --min 100 green (1762x12), the 4 new UI wiring tests (26 assertions), and 152 existing test_repo_invariants UI tests green (the 5 remaining test_repo_invariants failures are pre-existing sandbox dep gaps — fastapi/httpx/package-metadata — unrelated to this static-only change). Full pytest/mypy is CI-only (repo requires py3.13). REMAINING: human click-through across themes/breakpoints (fork-3); item-5 deletion in a browser-verified pass; item-2/3/4 JS strings are English-fallback via t() (keyable later); item 3's strip already existed (only the enlarge is new).
